stages.title: Dead code & deprecated audit — full codebase scan
stages.description:
Perform a thorough dead code and deprecated code audit of the Minions codebase (D:/squad). Analysis only — do NOT make any code changes.

Scan for:

**Deprecated shims (check docs/deprecated.json first)**
- Read docs/deprecated.json for all tracked deprecations
- For each entry, verify whether the shim/alias/fallback is still present in code
- Note any that are past their removal date or already cleaned

**Unreachable and dead code**
- Functions, methods, or branches that are defined but never called anywhere
- Exported symbols from engine/*.js or shared utilities that are never imported
- Feature flags or config checks that can never be true (e.g. checking for a field that is never written)
- Dead else-branches — conditions that always evaluate the same way given current data shapes
- Commented-out code blocks (multi-line // or /* */ blocks that look like disabled logic, not explanatory comments)

**Legacy patterns superseded by newer utilities**
- Direct fs.readFileSync/writeFileSync on shared state files that should use mutateJsonFileLocked
- Raw string status comparisons that should use WI_STATUS / WORK_TYPE constants
- process.env.HOME usage instead of os.homedir()
- proc.kill() calls instead of shared.killGracefully()/killImmediate()
- safeWrite() calls on files that are read-modify-written concurrently (race conditions)

**Dead-end features**
- Config fields read but never written (or vice versa)
- State file fields populated but never consumed anywhere
- Endpoints registered in the routes table but whose handlers always return early or no-op
- Scheduled tasks or pipelines that are disabled and have no path to re-enablement

**Stale references**
- Imports or requires of modules that no longer exist or export the referenced symbol
- References to files, paths, or directories that no longer exist
- Work item types, plan statuses, or PR statuses handled in switch/if chains but not present in constants

For each finding, record:
- File and line number(s)
- Category: deprecated-shim / dead-code / legacy-pattern / dead-feature / stale-reference
- Description of what it is and why it is dead/removable
- Safe to auto-remove or needs human judgment

Produce a structured findings report grouped by category. Be conservative — only flag something as dead if you are confident it is not used.

stages.title: Review dead code findings before planning
stages.description: The dead code audit is complete. Review the findings, then continue to generate cleanup work items.

stages.title: Dead code cleanup plan
stages.description:
Based on the dead code audit findings:

{{stages.dead-code-scan.output}}

Write a concrete implementation plan to remove the identified dead code. Each item should be:
- Scoped to a specific file and line range
- Safe to execute without breaking tests
- Ordered so dependent removals come after their prerequisites
- Marked as auto-remove (no judgment needed) or needs-review (human should verify)

Group items by category. For deprecated shims in docs/deprecated.json, include a step to remove the entry from that file after the shim is deleted.

Do not include items flagged as needing human judgment without a clear recommendation. The plan will be submitted for review before any execution begins — do not auto-approve.
